title: Latest comprehensive review remediation execution plan
outOfScope: production code and tests implementing the remediation work items
publishing a release or merging any future remediation PR

sources.reason: Latest comprehensive review findings and P0 development directions supplied by the user.
Repository-required Work Item lifecycle and finish criteria.
Canonical AI Cockpit governance loop and preflight behavior.
Existing plan structure and lifecycle wording to preserve.

riskAssessment.reason: This Work Item produces an execution plan only; it does not implement the remediation or perform release operations.

executionDecision.reason: The user supplied the review findings and explicitly requested a written execution plan.

preReviewWarnings: Review the proposed Work Item order and authorization wording before using the plan for implementation.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: The plan summarizes the review disposition, including strengths, P0 blockers, current release posture, and enterprise-readiness boundary.
The plan contains a non-overlapping ordered Work Item list, with the final two items exactly covering new-version release and execution-plan cleanup.

Every Work Item explicitly requires the complete lifecycle: latest remote base, dedicated branch, implementation, verification, Summary, ai-finish/archive, push, PR, merge, ai-close-work-item, and base synchronization before the next Work Item starts.
The plan records the user's authorization for the in-scope implementation and lifecycle actions, while identifying any genuinely external or human-only decision that must still be recorded.
guidelines: Keep the plan evidence-led: do not claim enterprise security or cryptographic guarantees that the review did not establish.
Preserve the Repository Governance Layer boundary; do not turn the plan into an Agent Runtime, Workflow Engine, or Security Sandbox specification.

intent.problem: The latest main-branch review found that the template has a credible governance structure and stronger release evidence, but the Trust Layer still cannot reliably prove fail-closed stopping across protocol enforcement, raw-intent interpretation, and multilingual/hidden-risk scenarios.
constraints: The requested output is an execution plan, not implementation of the remediation items.
The plan must end with Work Item 1: publish a new version and Work Item 2: clean execution-plan documents.
Work Items execute serially; the next one cannot begin until the previous one is fully closed.
Every claim must remain within the evidence and boundaries of the supplied review.
intent.rationale: A serial, lifecycle-complete plan prevents independent remediation branches from bypassing archive, PR, merge, closure, and release evidence, while making the remaining Trust Layer gaps independently testable.

problemStatement: Turn the latest comprehensive review into an ordered remediation backlog and an executable governance plan that can be followed one Work Item at a time until completion.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: The generated plan contains the review summary, ordered Work Items, final release and cleanup items, lifecycle loop, and authorization record.
The plan does not claim that enterprise security/compliance or the classic stop demo is already proven.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: Set only when a human explicitly approves restricted governance paths.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
